title: lappula albiflora
intro: Lappula albiflora, also known as whiteflower lappula, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, specifically in the Tian Shan and Pamir mountain ranges. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. In traditional medicine, Lappula albiflora is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption.
